description:
A backup-operator wants to prove that he can ❶ capture the exact list of
  Python packages in a virtual environment and then ❷ restore those same
  package versions later.  

  Your goal is to automate one proof-of-concept cycle and leave behind two
  verifiable artefacts:

  1. /home/user/backups/package_backup.txt  
     • MUST contain **exactly two lines**, in **alphabetical order**  
     • Each line is the canonical “package==version” pair shown below and
       nothing else (no blank lines, no comments).  
       Expected lines (already alphabetised):  
         numpy==1.24.4  
         requests==2.31.0

  2. /home/user/backups/restore_validation.log  
     • MUST contain **exactly four lines**, in the precise order and wording
       shown here (no extra whitespace, no additional output):  
         [RESTORE START]  
         requests==2.31.0 OK  
         numpy==1.24.4 OK  
         [RESTORE COMPLETE]

  The demonstration must be done with only a small sequence of shell
  commands (roughly three to four) that a human operator could execute
  manually:

  a. Create a new virtual environment located at /home/user/backup_test_env  
  b. Activate that environment and install the two required packages at the
     specific versions shown above.  
  c. Generate the backup file (step 1) using a single pip command.  
  d. Prove restoration ability by reinstalling from that backup list and
     writing the exact log file (step 2) that echoes a simple “OK” line for
     each package restored.

  Constraints & verification notes:

  • The directory /home/user/backups already exists and is writable.  
  • DO NOT use sudo; everything must run as the normal user.  
  • The test harness will only check the two files listed above, so do not
    delete them when you finish.  
  • The harness will compare the files byte-for-byte; follow the required
    wording and ordering strictly (case-sensitive, no trailing spaces).  

  When you are finished, both files should exist with precisely the
  contents defined above so the automatic grader can confirm success.
truth:
Expected file 1:
    Path: /home/user/backups/package_backup.txt
    Exact content (including final newlines):
      numpy==1.24.4\n
      requests==2.31.0\n

  Expected file 2:
    Path: /home/user/backups/restore_validation.log
    Exact content (including final newlines):
      [RESTORE START]\n
      requests==2.31.0 OK\n
      numpy==1.24.4 OK\n
      [RESTORE COMPLETE]\n
